violations.description: FOOD CONTACT SURFACES - CLEAN (2 pts): Food storage shelving clean dish/utensil shelving top of range reach-in cooler and freezer door handles and various prep areas in the kitchen noted not properly cleaned/maintained.

NON FOOD CONTACT SURFACES - CLEAN (1 pts): Exterior of bulk dry good bins (grey tubs) sides and back of fryer sides of equipment in the kitchen reach-in cooler doors reach-in cooler and freezer interiors throughout facility; all noted not clean.
VENTILATION HOOD SYSTEM (1 pts): Hood and filters noted not clean. They were observed to VERY full and dripping with grease and likely present a fire danger.

FLOORS WALLS CEILINGS (1 pts): Floors in the kitchen in the dry storage area in the mop sink area and especially in the back storage room; all noted in need of cleaning. Walls in the kitchen behind the prep cooler behind the hand sink in the ware-washing area and the mop sink area; all noted in need of cleaning. | FLOORS WALLS CEILINGS (1 pts): Floors and walls in the ware wash area noted in need of repair.

IN-USE UTENSILS (1 pts): In - use utensil noted being stored with the handle laying in the bulk dry goods. Also bowl noted being used for scoop in bulk dry goods. All scoops are to have handles and if stored in the food the handle must be pointed so as to not contact the food product.

FOOD DISPLAY SERVICE AND TRANSPORT TEMP (5 pts): Chicken noted not being held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. Tub of raw chicken to be prepped/cut noted sitting at room temperature too long. It was noted at 65?F. Refrigerated meats/foods must be prepared quickly enough to prevent temperature abuse. Temperature abuse is the raising and lowering of the holding temperature (above 41?F or below 140?F) which allows for the growth of potentially harmful bacteria. The chicken was disposed of to correct the critical violation.

SANITIZATION OF EQUIP. AND UTENSILS (4 pts): An inadequate amount of chlorine sanitizer was noted in the final rinse of the dish machine. The dish machine chlorine sanitizer had lost its prime. The machine's sanitizer line was primed to correct the violation. Daily testing of the dish machine's sanitizer concentration could help prevent future violations.
CONTAMINATION DURING STORAGE (2 pts): Container of sauce noted being stored on the floor in the furnace/mop sink room.
PREMISES (1 pts): Mops and brooms not hung.
FLOORS WALLS CEILINGS (1 pts): Ceiling vent covers and walls in the kitchen noted in need of cleaning. Floors in the ware wash area near the dish machine noted in need of repair.

IN-USE UTENSILS (1 pts): In - use utensil noted being stored with the handle laying in the dry rice. Also bowl noted being used for scoop in sugar. All scoops are to have handles and if stored in the food the handle must be pointed so as to not contact the food product.
LIGHTING (1 pts): Several light fixtures in the kitchen noted not functioning at all.
NON FOOD CONTACT SURFACES - CLEAN (1 pts): Reach-in white freezer handle sliding cooler door handles and shelving next to prep coolers noted not clean.
VENTILATION HOOD SYSTEM (1 pts): Vent hoods and filters noted not clean.
STORAGE/HANDLING CLEAN EQUIP & UTENSILS (1 pts): Wok noted stored in the mop sink. Please be aware that wok must be washed rinsed and sanitized ONLY in the 3-compartment sink.
